findings.evidence: Mathlib’s invariant-submodule API uses submodule order first: `Mathlib/Algebra/Module/Submodule/Invariant.lean:32` has `carrier := {p : Submodule R M | p ≤ p.comap f}` and `:48` exposes `p.map f ≤ p`; Mathlib’s tensor API already names this inclusion as `P.subtype.rTensor C` (`Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/TensorProduct/Map.lean:331`, `:338`) and uses ranges of it in tensor finiteness (`Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/TensorProduct/Finiteness.lean:132`).
findings.fix: Define it as `P.map (Comodule.coact (R := R) (C := C) (M := M)) ≤ LinearMap.range (P.subtype.rTensor C)` or equivalently `P ≤ (LinearMap.range (P.subtype.rTensor C)).comap ...`, and derive the elementwise iff as API.
findings.issue: `IsSubcomodule` is defined elementwise against a bespoke `submoduleCoactionRange`, instead of as the standard submodule-map stability statement.

summary: The stability condition is mathematically the right one, but it is exposed below the standard Mathlib submodule level.
